encounter.reason: Post-discharge heart-failure follow-up and medication reconciliation
encounter.service_provider: University of New Mexico Hospital and Heart Failure Clinic
encounter.location: UNM Heart Failure Clinic, Albuquerque, New Mexico

conditions.name: heart failure with reduced ejection fraction
conditions.supporting_text: improving after recent hospitalization, euvolemic on exam, symptoms improved, and functional status assessed as NYHA class II

conditions.name: New York Heart Association class II functional limitation due to heart failure
conditions.supporting_text: created to capture current heart-failure functional class during post-discharge reassessment

conditions.name: paroxysmal atrial fibrillation
conditions.supporting_text: remains rate/rhythm controlled, no current palpitations, tolerating anticoagulation without bleeding

conditions.name: chronic kidney disease stage 3b
conditions.supporting_text: renal function remained impaired but stable enough to continue current heart-failure regimen; creatinine, eGFR, and potassium reviewed

medications.name: carvedilol 12.5 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: take 12.5 mg by mouth twice daily
medications.reason: heart failure with reduced ejection fraction
medications.supporting_text: dose increased from 6.25 mg twice daily to 12.5 mg twice daily because blood pressure tolerated therapy and heart-failure status was improving

medications.name: sacubitril/valsartan 24/26 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: take 1 tablet by mouth twice daily
medications.reason: heart failure with reduced ejection fraction

medications.name: furosemide 40 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: take 40 mg by mouth each morning
medications.reason: heart failure with reduced ejection fraction / volume management

medications.name: apixaban 5 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: take 5 mg by mouth twice daily
medications.supporting_text: ongoing therapy confirmed; bleeding precautions reviewed and no bleeding reported

medications.name: empagliflozin 10 mg oral tablet
medications.reason: type 2 diabetes mellitus and heart failure with reduced ejection fraction

document_references.name: Progress note
document_references.description: Heart-failure clinic follow-up note documenting post-discharge symptom improvement, euvolemic examination, medication reconciliation, current vitals, and laboratory review. The note should summarize reassessment of HFrEF, atrial fibrillation, CKD stage 3b, and diabetes, and record the carvedilol dose increase with return precautions and follow-up plan.

document_references.name: Pharmacist medication access note
document_references.description: Medication management note documenting reconciliation of the chronic medication list, confirmation of active use, and support from the PharmD during transition of care. The note should include manufacturer copay card assistance, prescription transfer to a local Albuquerque pharmacy, and efforts to reduce future medication-access interruption.

document_references.name: Patient instructions
document_references.description: Written after-visit instructions reinforcing daily weight logging, medication adherence, sodium restriction, and return precautions for worsening dyspnea, orthopnea, edema, rapid weight gain, syncope, or bleeding. The instructions should also state that repeat labs are planned in 2 weeks and heart-failure/cardiology follow-up in 3 months.

document_references.name: Care plan update
document_references.description: Updated outpatient heart-failure management plan reflecting improving NYHA class II symptoms, current euvolemic status, continued guideline-directed medical therapy, and monitoring plan. The document should capture medication titration, planned repeat laboratory monitoring, and follow-up timing.

diagnostic_reports.description: same-day chemistry panel reviewed during the visit with partial named component results documented in the note, including creatinine, BUN, sodium, potassium, and bicarbonate. The narrative does not provide a full enough panel component set to reliably represent the complete panel as report-with-results.

diagnostic_reports.type: Repeat renal function and electrolyte laboratory testing
diagnostic_reports.description: repeat labs planned in 2 weeks to monitor renal function, electrolytes, and response to medication titration after carvedilol dose increase; exact panel composition not fully specified, but likely includes repeat chemistry testing relevant to CKD and heart-failure therapy
